title: India and Canada Sign Joint Energy Statement Focusing on Hydrogen, Biofuels, and SAF
content: At India Energy Week 2026 in Goa, India and Canada signed a joint statement to deepen bilateral energy cooperation, placing a significant emphasis on clean energy transition alongside conventional security. Indian Minister Hardeep Singh Puri and Canadian Minister Timothy Hodgson launched a renewed Ministerial Energy Dialogue, identifying hydrogen, biofuels, and Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) as key areas for collaboration to meet climate objectives. The Ministers recognized the immense potential in clean technology value chains, committing to support the deployment of these low-carbon fuels and battery storage solutions. The statement also highlighted ongoing efforts to advance global biofuel adoption through the Global Biofuels Alliance (GBA), where Canada serves as an observer. Both nations aim to facilitate reciprocal investment in these sectors, leveraging Canadaâ€™s role as a resource supplier and Indiaâ€™s position as a high-growth energy market to drive global decarbonization.
author: Analysts Account
